Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "expense tool" or "ExpenseBot" or "config" (or similar) using search_emails to find the full incident email chain. Expected findings: Samira's emails to Elena with the 47-expense audit summary (39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure), Elena's directive to pause auto-decisions and mandate a full audit, Samira's remediation plan with timeline, and Mina's escalation to Samira about Danielle Osei's $475 chair being wrongly rejected (originally flagged by Rachel Whitfield at Vectral).
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "policy" or "spreadsheet" or "expense categories" (or similar) using search_emails to find the config creation chain. Expected findings: Samira's original email to Chloe requesting operational requirements for the expense tool, Chloe's reply providing the source spreadsheet with per-client policy details (Vectral and Mosaic as pilot clients with different rules), and Samira's confirmation forwarding the spreadsheet to Lena for JSON config generation via the ingestion pipeline. ENG-209 is referenced in Linear issue descriptions as the pipeline ticket, not in this email itself.
OE 3: Search Slack with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense config" or "config failure" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find engineering discussions about the config bugs, pilot pause, and remediation. Expected findings: messages in C003 (#engineering) about the config fix deployments, schema validation tests, and launch readiness discussions. Also find Dmitri's comprehensive pilot audit post with the per-client breakdown (28 Vectral expenses, 19 Mosaic expenses).
OE 4: Retrieve history of channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_history to find the full technical conversation thread. Expected findings: Samira's launch readiness gate criteria (all validation tests passing, config data checked against source, AM approval), Dmitri's pre-launch warning about edge case coverage gaps (in the "Progress Update (Feb 27)" thread, actually posted on 2026-03-05), Lena's backlog triage message about deferring the invoice attachment safety ticket for DOT compliance work, Samira's message referencing the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket as still sitting in backlog after the Terraform pricing leak, and Lena's message about incorporating schema tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate.
OE 5: Search Linear for expense auto-categorizer project issues using linear_list_issues with query "expense" or "config" or "pipeline" (or similar), optionally filtered to project proj_expense_autocategorizer. Expected findings: ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, state_in_progress, assigned to Lena, P1), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, state_in_progress, assigned to Lena, P1), ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses, state_in_progress, assigned to Dmitri, Urgent), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening, state_in_progress, assigned to Lena, P1), ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog, state_in_progress, assigned to Dmitri), ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist, state_todo, assigned to Samira), ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, state_todo, assigned to Dmitri, due 3/25 overdue).
OE 6: Read Linear comments on issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) using linear_list_comments to find Lena's fix plan comment confirming she is updating all Vectral limits from default template settings to values confirmed by Chloe and eliminating the try/catch fallback. Read Linear comments on issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) using linear_list_comments to find Lena's fix plan confirming she is correcting excluded_categories to an array and max_amount fields to numbers, and implementing explicit type guards in PolicyValidator. Also find comment_lena_deploy_verified confirming both config corrections deployed and verified in production with specific test cases, and noting Kevin Tran's internet setup fee ($185 against $150 monthly limit) as a known remaining edge case.
OE 7: Read Linear comments on issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) using linear_list_comments. Expected findings: Dmitri's audit results (comment_dmitri_audit_results) posted here with the full 47-expense breakdown (39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 exposure). Also find remaining unchecked items in the ticket description acceptance criteria. The ticket is still state_in_progress with due date 3/20 (over 5 weeks past due).
OE 8: Read Linear comments on issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) using linear_list_comments, and read issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219) using linear_get_issue. Expected findings for ENG-212: comment_dmitri_validation_tests listing the 6 implemented schema validation tests (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback) with Lena incorporating these into the ENG-217 deployment gate, plus remaining unchecked items including vendor abbreviation handling and internet setup vs monthly charge distinction. Expected findings for ENG-219: state_todo, assigned to Samira, due 3/21 (over 5 weeks overdue), proposed checklist criteria from Samira's 3/18 remediation post but no comments or updates since creation.
OE 9: Search Airtable stipend records for Vectral Systems using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550 standing desk, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82 keyboard tray, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130 mesh WiFi, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"). Also find recStip000000016 (Danielle Osei, $475, Approved by Mina), recStip000000017 (Kevin Tran, $185, Approved by Mina). Also find duplicate records rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk ($550, Pending Approval) and rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi ($130, Pending Approval) that also need correction.
OE 10: Search Airtable stipend records for Mosaic Robotics using airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Account", value "Mosaic" (or similar). Expected findings: recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, $1,200 standing desk, Approved in error due to string comparison bug), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, $350 coworking membership, Approved in error due to excluded_categories string bug), recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, $135 coworking day passes, Approved in error due to same exclusion bug). Also find rec_stipend_andre_keyboard ($89 keyboard, Pending Approval, flagged as duplicate receipt hash matching Kevin Tran).
OE 11: Search Linear for the invoice attachment safety ticket using linear_list_issues with query "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" (or similar). Expected finding: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 ("Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches", number 187, state_backlog, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, due date 2026-04-18, created 2026-03-18). This is the deprioritized validation gap Samira referenced.
OE 12: Read comments on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 using linear_list_comments. Expected findings: Lena's deferral comment (linear_comment_77eafc95e169, 2026-04-06) stating she is pushing back the ticket for DOT compliance work and leaving it unassigned for Samira to re-slot. Samira's response (linear_comment_3dfd1f1dd151, 2026-04-06) expressing discomfort with leaving it in backlog and noting intent to revisit after DOT. Samira's escalation comment (linear_comment_f0c737166dc1, 2026-04-15) framing this as a second pricing-exposure event after Terraform and requesting an owner and stopgap measures. Marcus's comment (linear_comment_948885261d6e, 2026-04-15) flagging the live consequence - the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap on 2026-04-10 where QuickBooks records were correct but emailed PDFs were mismatched between clients, resulting in pricing exposure. Marcus's follow-up (linear_comment_76c40fb4953f, 2026-04-22) requesting engineering reactivate the dormant safeguard work with attachment verification or a manual checkpoint.
OE 13: Search Slack or emails for Samira's explicit connection between the invoice safety ticket and the ExpenseBot config failures. Using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "attachment mismatch" or "Terraform pricing leak" or "same class of risk" (or similar) in C003 (#engineering), or using search_emails with query "attachment safety" or "exact class of risk" (or similar). Expected finding: Samira's message in #engineering (ts: 1776345000) stating the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket opened after the Terraform pricing leak is still in backlog with no owner, and connecting it to the same systemic pattern of missing validation gates. Also find Samira's email to Marcus and David stating "we had already identified this exact class of risk after the Terraform pricing exposure and opened a ticket for invoice attachment safety checks."
OE 14: Search Linear for duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_issues with query "duplicate" or "receipt" or "hash" (or similar). Read comments on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) using linear_list_comments. Expected findings: ticket is state_todo, due date 2026-03-25 (over a month overdue), assigned to Dmitri. The description covers Layer 1 (hash dedup) and Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity scoring), plus the $100 manual review threshold from Elena's directive (issued around 2026-04-08 in Slack ts:1775613900). The ENG-220 creation date is 2026-03-15, which predates the directive. Dmitri's comment on ENG-220 (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context) provides filing context linking to ENG-216, Airtable IDs, QuickBooks holds, and AM outreach. The detailed duplicate discovery findings (the 3 flagged receipts - Kevin Tran $89 keyboard and Andre Washington $89 keyboard with identical SHA-256 hashes, plus Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand with matching PDF template metadata) are in comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery on issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), not on ENG-220.
OE 15: Update the 3 stale Vectral stipend records in Airtable using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001. Update recStip000000018 (Maria Santos) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 550. Update recStip000000019 (James Okafor) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 82. Update recStip000000020 (Lisa Park) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 130. Review Notes should reference the original auto-rejection bug and the correction per audit findings.
OE 16: Update the duplicate Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001. Update rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 550. Update rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 130. No duplicate record exists for James Okafor.
OE 17: Update stale Linear ticket states using linear_update_issue. Close issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) to Done - the audit is complete. Close issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) to Done - the Vectral config fix was deployed. Close issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) to Done - the Mosaic config fix was deployed. Do NOT close issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) - it is still in_progress with unconfirmed acceptance criteria.
OE 18: Add a post-mortem findings comment to issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) using linear_create_comment. Content should assess which acceptance criteria from the ticket description are confirmed deployed (silent fallback removal, type enforcement) versus unconfirmed (schema validation gate integration into deployment pipeline, config diff logging). The pipeline has only been validated against 2 pilot clients and new client onboarding would exercise untested paths.
OE 19: Add a schema test coverage gap analysis comment to issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) using linear_create_comment. Content should map the 6 implemented tests against the specific pilot failure modes and identify uncovered gaps: categorization keyword completeness (James Okafor keyboard tray miscategorized as office_supplies), internet setup vs monthly charge distinction (Kevin Tran $185 setup fee against $150 monthly limit), cross-client duplicate receipt detection (ENG-220 still in todo), $100 manual review threshold (Elena's directive not implemented), and remaining unchecked items (vendor abbreviation handling, bundled item descriptions).
OE 20: Add an overdue status check comment to issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) using linear_create_comment. Content should note the ticket is over a month past its 3/25 due date with no implementation started. The current Airtable status of flagged receipts: Kevin Tran $89 (paid out), Andre Washington $89 (still Pending Approval), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (still Pending Approval). The $100 manual review threshold from Elena's directive is also scoped in this ticket but not implemented.
OE 21: Post a comprehensive post-mortem summary in channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_add_message. Content should cover: (1) root cause - how configs were generated wrong by the policy ingestion pipeline, distinguishing the Vectral bug (silent fallback to default template limits due to merged cell parse error) from the Mosaic bug (malformed JSON types - string vs number for max_amount, string vs array for excluded_categories), (2) what broke per client - 5 Vectral false rejections (Danielle Osei $475, Kevin Tran $185, Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130) and 3 Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking, total $885 exposure), (3) stipend record fix status - 3 records found still unfixed and corrected today, (4) assessment of Lena's fixes - structural prevention vs value patches, (5) schema test coverage gaps, (6) the deprioritized validation gap - the invoice attachment safety ticket (ENG-187) that ties to the same systemic pattern of missing validation gates, and (7) relaunch blockers for Samira's conversation with Elena.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "attachment mismatch" or "Terraform pricing leak" or "same class of risk" (or similar) in C003 (#engineering), or using search_emails with query "attachment safety" or "exact class of risk" (or similar). Expected finding: Samira's message in #engineering (ts: 1776345000) stating the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket opened after the Terraform pricing leak is still in backlog with no owner, and connecting it to the same systemic pattern of missing validation gates. Also find Samira's email to Marcus and David stating "we had already identified this exact class of risk after the Terraform pricing exposure and opened a ticket for invoice attachment safety checks."
OE 14: Search Linear for duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_issues with query "duplicate" or "receipt" or "hash" (or similar). Read comments on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) using linear_list_comments. Expected findings: ticket is state_todo, due date 2026-03-25 (over a month overdue), assigned to Dmitri. The description covers Layer 1 (hash dedup) and Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity scoring), plus the $100 manual review threshold from Elena's directive (issued around 2026-04-08 in Slack ts:1775613900). The ENG-220 creation date is 2026-03-15, which predates the directive. Dmitri's comment on ENG-220 (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context) provides filing context linking to ENG-216, Airtable IDs, QuickBooks holds, and AM outreach. The detailed duplicate discovery findings (the 3 flagged receipts - Kevin Tran $89 keyboard and Andre Washington $89 keyboard with identical SHA-256 hashes, plus Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand with matching PDF template metadata) are in comment_dmitri_duplicate_discovery on issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), not on ENG-220.
OE 15: Update the 3 stale Vectral stipend records in Airtable using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001. Update recStip000000018 (Maria Santos) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 550. Update recStip000000019 (James Okafor) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 82. Update recStip000000020 (Lisa Park) to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 130. Review Notes should reference the original auto-rejection bug and the correction per audit findings.
OE 16: Update the duplicate Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblStipends00001. Update rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 550. Update rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi to Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: 130. No duplicate record exists for James Okafor.
OE 17: Update stale Linear ticket states using linear_update_issue. Close issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216) to Done - the audit is complete. Close issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214) to Done - the Vectral config fix was deployed. Close issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215) to Done - the Mosaic config fix was deployed. Do NOT close issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) - it is still in_progress with unconfirmed acceptance criteria.
OE 18: Add a post-mortem findings comment to issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) using linear_create_comment. Content should assess which acceptance criteria from the ticket description are confirmed deployed (silent fallback removal, type enforcement) versus unconfirmed (schema validation gate integration into deployment pipeline, config diff logging). The pipeline has only been validated against 2 pilot clients and new client onboarding would exercise untested paths.
OE 19: Add a schema test coverage gap analysis comment to issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212) using linear_create_comment. Content should map the 6 implemented tests against the specific pilot failure modes and identify uncovered gaps: categorization keyword completeness (James Okafor keyboard tray miscategorized as office_supplies), internet setup vs monthly charge distinction (Kevin Tran $185 setup fee against $150 monthly limit), cross-client duplicate receipt detection (ENG-220 still in todo), $100 manual review threshold (Elena's directive not implemented), and remaining unchecked items (vendor abbreviation handling, bundled item descriptions).
OE 20: Add an overdue status check comment to issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220) using linear_create_comment. Content should note the ticket is over a month past its 3/25 due date with no implementation started. The current Airtable status of flagged receipts: Kevin Tran $89 (paid out), Andre Washington $89 (still Pending Approval), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (still Pending Approval). The $100 manual review threshold from Elena's directive is also scoped in this ticket but not implemented.
OE 21: Post a comprehensive post-mortem summary in channel C003 (#engineering) using conversations_add_message. Content should cover: (1) root cause - how configs were generated wrong by the policy ingestion pipeline, distinguishing the Vectral bug (silent fallback to default template limits due to merged cell parse error) from the Mosaic bug (malformed JSON types - string vs number for max_amount, string vs array for excluded_categories), (2) what broke per client - 5 Vectral false rejections (Danielle Osei $475, Kevin Tran $185, Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130) and 3 Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking, total $885 exposure), (3) stipend record fix status - 3 records found still unfixed and corrected today, (4) assessment of Lena's fixes - structural prevention vs value patches, (5) schema test coverage gaps, (6) the deprioritized validation gap - the invoice attachment safety ticket (ENG-187) that ties to the same systemic pattern of missing validation gates, and (7) relaunch blockers for Samira's conversation with Elena.

1 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_comments) to retrieve expense auto-categorizer project ticket data.

19 The message posted to C003 (#engineering) describes the Vectral config bug root cause (silent fallback to default template limits due to a parse error or ingestion pipeline failure, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #engineering message explicitly describes: 'Vectral Systems: A merged cell in the ergonomic column caused a parse error. The pipeline's try/catch swallowed the error and fell back to default template values.' This clearly describes the silent fallback to default template limits.
✓Run #2The engineering message explicitly states: 'The pipeline's try/catch block silently swallowed the error and fell back to the default template values' for Vectral — clearly describing the silent fallback root cause.
